concerns.text: FR-003 contains vague phrase 'high match threshold' for algorithm validation. MAINTAINER DECISION requires explicit quantification: '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values'. This is a writing issue (unclear requirement statement) not scope drift.

FR-013 states 'Reference coverage threshold set to a high level'. MAINTAINER DECISION requires explicit quantification: '≥ 90% of cited references resolve to a verified source'. This is a writing issue not scope drift.

SC-010 states 'achieves a high match threshold for pass/fail status'. MAINTAINER DECISION requires explicit quantification: '≥ 90% match against reference values'. This is a writing issue not scope drift.

Constitution Principle VII now includes the census-data exception amendment (2026-06-14). This resolves the prior FR-006 vs Constitution contradiction. However, verify this amendment text matches exactly what was specified in the MAINTAINER DECISION.

FR-003 still contains excessive inline caveats ('Phase 1 Implementation Exclusion', 'Phase 2+ Timeline', 'Threshold Distinction', etc.). MAINTAINER DECISION states: 'Remove FR-003's excessive inline caveats (keep one concise Phase-1 note)'. These should be moved to Assumptions or a dedicated Phase 2+ section.

SC-005 states 'The vast majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' - MAINTAINER DECISION requires this to be '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Current text uses unquantified 'vast majority'.
SC-010 states 'achieves a high match threshold for pass/fail status' - MAINTAINER DECISION requires '≥ 90% match against reference values'. Current text uses unquantified 'high match threshold'.

SC-011 states 'families with residuals exceeding a specified threshold' - MAINTAINER DECISION requires 'families with residuals exceeding ≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend'. Current text uses unquantified 'specified threshold'.
FR-013 states 'Reference coverage threshold set to a high level' - MAINTAINER DECISION requires '≥ 90% of cited references resolve to a verified source'. Current text uses unquantified 'high level'.

FR-003 still states 'Substantial match against KnotInfo reference values' instead of the MAINTAINER DECISION quantification '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values'. Prior concern scope-54717837 remains unresolved.

SC-005 states 'The vast majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of MAINTAINER DECISION quantification '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. New unquantified phrase introduced.

concerns.text: SC-005 still uses 'The overwhelming majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of the maintainer-decided threshold '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Per maintainer decision, this vague phrase must be replaced verbatim with the quantified value.

SC-011 still uses 'families with residuals exceeding a significant threshold of standard deviations from the fitted trend' instead of the maintainer-decided 'families with residuals exceeding ≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend'. Per maintainer decision, this vague phrase must be replaced verbatim with the quantified value.

SC-005 states 'The vast majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' - MAINTAINER DECISION requires this to be '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Current text uses unquantified 'vast majority'.
SC-010 states 'achieves a high match threshold for pass/fail status' - MAINTAINER DECISION requires '≥ 90% match against reference values'. Current text uses unquantified 'high match threshold'.

SC-011 states 'families with residuals exceeding a specified threshold' - MAINTAINER DECISION requires 'families with residuals exceeding ≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend'. Current text uses unquantified 'specified threshold'.

SC-005 states 'The overwhelming majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' — MAINTAINER DECISION requires '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Current text uses unquantified 'The overwhelming majority' instead of the mandated threshold.

SC-011 states 'System MUST identify families with residuals exceeding a significant threshold of standard deviations from the fitted trend' — MAINTAINER DECISION requires 'families with residuals exceeding ≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend'. Current text uses unquantified 'significant threshold' instead of the mandated numeric value.

FR-013 Validation Threshold states 'System MUST achieve a substantial match against KnotInfo reference values' — this remains unquantified. While Reference Coverage Threshold is now ≥ 90%, the Validation Threshold itself still uses vague 'substantial match' language instead of a numeric threshold (e.g., ≥ 90% match).

SC-005 states 'The overwhelming majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' — 'overwhelming majority' is not quantified. Per maintainer decision, must specify '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'.

SC-011 states 'residuals exceeding a significant threshold of standard deviations from the fitted trend' — 'significant threshold' is not quantified. Per maintainer decision, must specify '≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend'.

FR-003 Phase 2+ scope boundary section states 'high match threshold' for algorithm validation against KnotInfo — not quantified. Per maintainer decision, must specify '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values'.
Constitution Principle VII now includes the census-data exception amendment (2026-06-14), resolving the internal_consistency-70324895 contradiction. This concern is now RESOLVED.
FR-001 now explicitly states '≤ 13 crossings' for download scope, resolving the predefined complexity limit ambiguity. This concern is now RESOLVED.
FR-013 now explicitly states '≥ 90% of cited references resolve to a verified source' for reference coverage threshold, resolving the vague 'high level' phrasing. This concern is now RESOLVED.

FR-003 still states 'Substantial match against KnotInfo reference values' instead of the MAINTAINER DECISION quantification '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values'. Prior concern scope-54717837 remains unresolved.

SC-005 still states 'The overwhelming majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' instead of MAINTAINER DECISION quantification '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated'. Reviser claimed to change this but the text remains unquantified.

SC-005 states 'The overwhelming majority of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' — 'overwhelming majority' is not quantified. Per MAINTAINER DECISION, must specify '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' for testability.

SC-010 states 'achieves a high match threshold for pass/fail status' — 'high match threshold' is not defined. Per MAINTAINER DECISION, must specify '≥ 90% match against reference values' for verifiability.

SC-011 states 'families with residuals exceeding a significant threshold of standard deviations' — 'significant threshold' is not defined. Per MAINTAINER DECISION, must specify '≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend' for testability.

FR-006 states 'Constitution Principle VII is satisfied via effect sizes with confidence intervals for census data.' Constitution Principle VII explicitly states 'p-values and confidence intervals are NOT required'. Spec claims CIs are required for satisfaction; Constitution says they are not. This is a direct contradiction that must be resolved by removing 'with confidence intervals' from FR-006 or amending Constitution to require CIs (which contradicts 'NOT required').

new_concerns.text: FR-006 states 'Constitution Principle VII is satisfied via effect sizes with confidence intervals for census data.' Constitution Principle VII explicitly states 'p-values and confidence intervals are NOT required'. Spec claims CIs are required for satisfaction; Constitution says they are not. This is a direct contradiction that must be resolved by removing 'with confidence intervals' from FR-006 or amending Constitution to require CIs (which contradicts 'NOT required').
